title: Plan emits a file manifest so downstream stages stop re-surveying the repo
description:
## Problem

Every stage is a separate cold `claude -p` process (`adw/invoke.py` — no
`--resume`, no shared session), so plan -> implement -> test -> review each
re-explore the codebase from scratch. Measured 2026-06-22: GH-56's plan stage was
**26,385 tokens**, of which ~90% was the agent's own `Read`/`Grep`/`Glob` (the
fixed scaffolding is only ~2K). The downstream stages re-pay similar reads at full
price. This repeated exploration is the dominant per-ticket token cost.

## Approach

- The plan stage emits a **machine-readable file manifest** in its status block:
  the files it will edit plus the key files to read for context (it already cites
  `file:line` refs in prose; formalize them).
- `compose_stage_prompt` (`adw/workflow_runner.py`) inlines the latest plan's
  manifest directly into the implement/test/review prompts (today it only lists
  prior-output *paths* to `Read`).
- Command files (IMPLEMENT/TEST/REVIEW) gain a rule: "the plan names the exact
  files; open only those, do not survey the codebase. If the manifest is wrong or
  insufficient, you may read more and say so." (Graceful degradation.)
acceptance_criteria: The plan status block carries a structured file manifest (files-to-edit +
Implement/test/review prompts inline that manifest; downstream stages are told
A representative ticket shows reduced downstream-stage reads (token usage is
Full suite stays green (`uv run pytest -q`).
